The greatest mistake Of Hposerelt
career was when he ran ~ steam
roller over the Chicago convention
and squeezed out the nomination
Of William H. Taft. ! do not be
lieve in the perpetuation OF po-
litical dynasties by the party "A
power, as is being attempted By
the present administration"


Judge Turner commented the
guarantee OF bank deposits. which,
he said, had been shown would not
cost bankers to exceed one tenth
of one per cent of their deposits
to put In operation. He said he
had lost all patience with bankers.
who after having enjoyed special
privileges for 40 years now try To
deny this boon to the people.
= He gave clear explanation of
the democratic attitude on the writ
of injunction, and said this position
was right legally and In accordance
with common law. Capital, he
said, is now trying to. deny to la
Dor the right of organization. which
it has enjoyed SO freely and to
such great advantage itself in late
years.


r. l.. McWilliams ana z. B.
Keeney also spoke and helped to
add to the enthusiasm, Mr..
Keeney delighted the audience
with his wit and philosophy, a com
bination that he possesses by ma
ture in a high degree. .g


Fred Schade presided over the
meeting and was rendered proud
by high compliment from Judge
Turner on the good work d6ne by
the young men in this campaign.
